rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the correct scope: component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations, and implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings
Use only visible identifiers in AGREE expressions, avoiding implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
generic_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid scopes according to the component type or implementation context
Use only visible identifiers in AGREE expressions to maintain consistency and avoid scope-related issues
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` syntax for AGREE contracts
anti_patterns: Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
